sections.narrative: Access control is BraidDB's strongest differentiator, backed by 141 CBAC unit tests, 92 RDKC tests, 21 EACB tests, 20 CBAC-RDKC integration tests, and 352 adversarial gym test functions across 28 files. CBAC implements a 6-state governance circuit breaker (Open, Restricted, Suspended, Quarantined, Sealed, EngagementAuthorized) with compare-and-swap semantics. REWG derives entitlements from graph position via bitwise AND — no ACL tables, no policy engine. EACB provides FROST threshold signatures for LAWS 3000.09 human-in-the-loop authorization. Access control is cryptographic: RDKC derives unique per-page keys from governance state. A CUI-clearance entity cannot derive the key for a SECRET page — verified by gym_g01 clearance boundary battery and gym_p01 pre-redaction leakage proof. ML-DSA-87 (the signature algorithm used throughout access control) passed NIST's live ACVP validation server on 2026-06-16 (session 744723, CNSA 2.0 Cat-5) — prior ACVP gap is closed. Gap to 9.5: FIPS 140-3 CMVP module certification (algorithm correctness now NIST-graded; module wrapper submission is the next step) and timing side-channel proof on target hardware.

evidence.label: CBAC Module (141 source tests; 206 total with test files)

history.note: ML-DSA-87 NIST ACVP live pass (session 744723, CNSA 2.0 Cat-5). ACVP gap closed; CMVP module submission is next.
141 CBAC, 92 RDKC, 21 EACB, 352 gym functions across 28 files. 25,343 security LOC. 94 modules. Gap at time: ML-DSA-87, FIPS ACVP (both now closed).

benchmarks.name: WAL Tamper Protection
benchmarks.value: HMAC-SHA384 chained format (FabricRS PR #247) — 33 WAL tests total. Per-record WalProvenance. ACVP test vectors (RFC 4231)
benchmarks.source: FabricRS PR #247. Chained HMAC: each tag = HMAC(key, prev_tag || provenance || data). WalProvenance: 26 bytes per record. CNSA 2.0 compliant.

benchmarks.name: In-Transit Payload Encryption (X-Wing-1024, Cat-5)
benchmarks.value: X-Wing-1024 (X25519 + ML-KEM-1024) payload encapsulation wired into exchange layer — PRs #2342 (peer-mesh sealing), #2344 (GNSE type-bound AAD), #2348 (ML-DSA-87 signed auto key-exchange). Cat-5 end-to-end.

comparisons.value: No in-transit payload-level PQC at the database layer

benchmarks.name: Transport-Layer PQC TLS (X25519MLKEM768 hybrid KEX)
benchmarks.value: pqc-tls-terminator crate (#2350) — rustls + aws-lc-rs, X25519MLKEM768 hybrid key exchange. Live on braidera.com (verified via openssl s_client). Precision: Cat-3 KEX (ML-KEM-768); cert auth still classical (no WebPKI PQC certs exist); api.digital-fabric.com ALB is classical (deploy gap, not code gap).

comparisons.value: No database ships its own PQC TLS terminator

benchmarks.name: NIST ACVP Algorithm Validation
benchmarks.value: ML-KEM-1024 (keyGen + encapDecap) + ML-DSA-87 (sigVer) passed on NIST's live ACVP server — session 744723, isSample=false, CNSA 2.0 Category-5 (2026-06-16). Offline KAT 35/35.

benchmarks.name: FIPS 140-3 Module Certification (CMVP)
benchmarks.value: Not yet submitted — ACVP algorithm validation complete; next step is module boundary definition + accredited lab engagement (CMVP queue)

sections.narrative: BraidDB is post-quantum end-to-end across all four data-protection legs — the only database to achieve this. Leg 1 (at rest): AES-256-GCM-SIV per-page encryption + ML-KEM-1024 key encapsulation, KAT-verified. Leg 2 (write integrity): ML-DSA-87 + SIS lattice accumulator + R-DPO causal chain, KAT-verified. Leg 3 (in-transit payload, Cat-5): X-Wing-1024 (X25519 + ML-KEM-1024) encapsulation wired into the exchange layer (PRs #2342/#2344/#2348) — every message sealed at Cat-5, independent of transport. Leg 4 (TLS KEX, hybrid Cat-3): X25519MLKEM768 via rustls + aws-lc-rs in the pqc-tls-terminator crate (PR #2350), live on braidera.com. NIST ACVP live pass (2026-06-16, session 744723): ML-KEM-1024 keyGen/encapDecap + ML-DSA-87 sigVer all graded passing, CNSA 2.0 Category-5. Offline KAT 35/35. Two precision points that keep this bulletproof under scrutiny: (a) TLS KEX is ML-KEM-768 (Cat-3 ceiling of the IETF hybrid draft); payload layer is Cat-5 (ML-KEM-1024). (b) Certificate authentication in TLS is still classical — no WebPKI PQC certs exist yet; say 'hybrid PQC TLS key exchange', not 'fully PQC TLS'. Deploy gap (not code): api.digital-fabric.com terminates at the AWS ALB which refuses PQC cipher suites; the pqc-tls-terminator is the path to close it. Implementation baseline: 125,181 LOC, 527 files, 84 modules, 451 unit tests + 352 adversarial gym functions across 14 security modules. Gap to 10.0: FIPS 140-3 CMVP module certification + closing the ALB deploy gap.

history.note: All four PQC legs complete. In-transit payload: X-Wing-1024 Cat-5 (PRs #2342/#2344/#2348). TLS KEX: X25519MLKEM768 live on braidera.com (PR #2350). NIST ACVP live pass (session 744723).
NIST ACVP live pass: ML-KEM-1024 keyGen/encapDecap + ML-DSA-87 sigVer (session 744723, CNSA 2.0 Cat-5). Offline KAT 35/35.
125K LOC, 527 files, 84 modules. 15,913 security LOC, 451 unit tests, 352 gym functions. 14 security modules.

benchmarks.name: WAL HMAC Chain (Tamper-Proof Audit)
benchmarks.value: HMAC-SHA384 chained format (FabricRS PR #247) — 33 WAL tests total. Per-record WalProvenance (agent_id, clearance, connectivity, timestamp). ACVP vectors (RFC 4231)
benchmarks.source: FabricRS PR #247. Chained HMAC: each tag = HMAC(key, prev_tag || provenance || data). WalProvenance: 26 bytes per record (NIST AU-10 non-repudiation). Static or RDKC-derived key modes. CNSA 2.0 compliant.

benchmarks.name: SSP Evidence Appendix (NIST SP 800-53)
benchmarks.value: GYM-R01 — automated control mapping from 28 gym files to 9 NIST control families

benchmarks.name: Compliance Export (SOX/HIPAA/NIST)
benchmarks.value: ComplianceReport with 7 control attestations (PR #250). SOX 404, HIPAA 164.312(b)/(c)(1), NIST AU-2/3/10/12

benchmarks.name: SIEM Integration (CEF/STIX)
benchmarks.value: CEF for ArcSight/Splunk + STIX 2.1 for Sentinel/Elastic (PR #250). 4 event types.

sections.narrative: Auditing is the most complete pillar at 9.5. ProvenanceChain tracks who/what/when on every entity at zero overhead. TCI discovers implicit relationships at flush time. CBAC audit trail records every state change with CAS-versioned immutability. WAL HMAC-SHA384 chained format (FabricRS PR #247) provides tamper-proof audit with per-record provenance (agent_id, clearance, connectivity, timestamp) — 26 bytes overhead, 33 tests, ACVP vectors for NIST validation prep. Compliance export (PR #250) covers SOX 404, HIPAA, NIST AU controls. SIEM integration outputs CEF for Splunk/ArcSight and STIX 2.1 for Sentinel/Elastic. GYM-R01 SSP Evidence maps 28 gym files to 9 NIST SP 800-53 control families. The only gap to 10.0: FIPS 140-3 lab certification of the HMAC implementation (process, not engineering).

history.note: Updated test counts. WAL HMAC chain (FabricRS PR #247), compliance export + SIEM (PR #250), GYM-R01 SSP evidence all on main.
